Title
Replay System Prompt

Purpose
Add a lightweight replay/input recording system to Shadow Shift for debugging, tuning, regression testing, and combat/movement analysis.

Why This Matters for V2
Replays are powerful engineering tools. They help reproduce bugs, compare tuning changes, and validate difficult platforming/combat interactions.

Detailed Codex Instructions
Context:
Shadow Shift already has nuanced movement and combat. Replay support would make future balancing and debugging far more reliable.

Goal:
Implement a deterministic-enough input recording and playback system suitable for local development and tuning.

Implementation Steps:
1. Audit input handling, timing assumptions, and save/reset flow to determine what must be captured.
2. Record player inputs, key state changes, timestamps or frame indices, and seed/state context needed for playback.
3. Add a replay playback mode that feeds recorded input back into the game.
4. Provide simple developer controls for start/stop recording and playback.
5. Document any determinism limits clearly.

Constraints:
- Keep the system lightweight
- Do not assume perfect determinism unless the runtime supports it
- Focus on practical debugging value

Implementation Notes
- Frame-indexed input logs are often enough for a browser action game.
- Pairing replay with room spawn/reset tools is especially useful.
- If full replay fidelity is hard, prioritize reproducibility of core movement/combat scenarios.

Validation Checklist
- Inputs can be recorded and replayed
- Common movement/combat sequences reproduce reliably enough for testing
- Developer controls are clear
- The feature helps debugging and tuning
- Replay code is isolated and maintainable
